items.park_reason: Canonical jump-table infrastructure, not a register/codegen cheat. The 24 asmfix rules (15 renames of GCC .L labels to external jtbl_800108CC + 8 jlabel conversions + 1 delete_between stripping GCC's emitted table) are structurally required: the switch's jump table jtbl_800108CC sits in the FIRST rodata block (asm/data 800.rodata_pre.o, bb2.ld:25, addr 0x800108CC), while code6cac_b2.o's own .rodata links far later (bb2.ld:50). A GCC in-object jump table can only land in code6cac_b2's late rodata, never at 0x108CC, so referencing the external jtbl symbol + deleting GCC's table is mandatory. Zero-asmfix COMPLETED-C would need relocating the jtbl into code6cac_b2.c AND reordering the whole global rodata layout (project-wide, needs user sign-off). This is the project standard for the 26 jtbl-rename functions whose tables splat carved into asm/data rodata. Separately, sandbox cannot score this file: file-wide cheat-asm stripping shifts maspsx indices and crashes a sibling fn's index-based regfix reorder rule (KeyError in tools/regfix.py).

Prologue-fix coupled cheat: register asm pin + asm barrier on s1 force EARLY materialization (move s1,a2 at insn 4 in prologue). Without them GCC defers to insn 12 (after early-exit check). prologue_config.json entry can't fix missing-early-mat. Need deeper rework per prologue-fix-redundant-reorder.md to retire both cheats together. Pure-C floor 2 (verified score=2 with pins removed).

Cheat-asm pins forced TWO separate li -1 materializations into  and  (one per nested do-while compare against -1). GCC's natural CSE folds them to ONE shared register. Removing pins drops 1 instruction (build=34 vs target=35). The redundant li was load-bearing for the match. Pure-C lever to force two materializations not yet found; needs deeper analysis.

File-scope cheat: extern volatile s32 D_8009BF7C; at display.c:934 forces D_8009BF7C reloads in this function (lines 981, 990). Body itself is clean pure-C, score 0. Unblocking requires removing the volatile decl (file-wide effect) which would change codegen for all D_8009BF7C-using functions in display.c. Multi-function refactor — not a quick win.

Heavy cheat load: 4 register-asm pins (i/c/tmp/p), 1 asm move-via-output (addu), 3 inline volatile casts on game-state (((volatile u8 *)p)[8]=0; etc.). The volatile coercions force GCC to emit separate byte stores instead of combining adjacent bytes. Not a quick pin-removal — needs structural rework to produce separate stores from clean C (struct-of-bytes, etc.).

File-scope cheat: extern volatile s32 D_800A2634; at ings2.c:321. D_800A2634 is incremented by interrupt-callback D_800832F8 and read here — semantically REQUIRES volatile for interrupt-safety, but project policy classifies volatile-on-game-state as a cheat regardless. Function body is otherwise clean. Resolution needs a project-wide policy carve-out for genuine ISR-shared variables, or file-scope coordinated cleanup.

File-scope cheats: extern volatile s32 D_800A28A0 and D_800A2CD4 elsewhere in main.c. Function body is clean pure-C. Same file-scope-coordinated-cleanup pattern affecting multiple distance-0 queue items in this batch.

Cheats: volatile s32 sp_pad (frame coercion: target frame=32, removing drops to 24) + register asm pin. Frame coercion via volatile-local is FORBIDDEN per [[dead-vars-local-array]] but the function has no natural reason to allocate a 32-byte frame. The sp_pad was load-bearing for the byte match. Needs structural rework to find a real reason for the larger frame.

Frame-coercion cheats: s32 dummy[2] (unused-local-array, name announces intent) and char new_var4 (declared, never assigned, only (void)-discarded). Both FORBIDDEN per [[dead-vars-local-array]]. Used to reserve frame bytes. Function has no natural use for these locals — removing them would shrink frame and break byte match.
Same file-scope-volatile-extern pattern: function body is clean pure-C but blocked by 2 file-scope cheats in text1b.c. Needs coordinated file-wide volatile-decl cleanup.

Re-derivation 2026-06-02: pure-C without forbidden DImode chain bottoms at sandbox distance 9 (build_insns 81 matches target, but 9 register-rotation+scheduling diffs persist). HEAD's source uses 'unsigned long long new_var2; new_var2 = new_var; count = new_var2;' DImode chain which is FORBIDDEN per the 2026-06-02 policy (same family as combine-foldable chain-extender in register-alloc-pure-c.md, classified cheat-by-spelling — zero semantic purpose, exists solely to bump count's RTL refs and shift cc1's allocno priority). The 4 regfix substs at @50/@56 (ddiu->addu, zero,-1->0,zero,  eq->bne, s0,v0->v0,zero) themselves manufacture the FORBIDDEN goto-end+ret_val accumulator pattern (per goto-end-prologue-delay-slot.md archive): the cheat-free source's natural 'if (count == -1) return 0' is semantically equivalent to target's accumulator-based 'v0=0; goto check; v0=-1; check: if (v0!=0) return 0' but produces different bytes (li v0,-1 in the beqz delay slot + beq s0,v0 post-loop). Tried levers (all measured negative from baseline 9): decl order swaps (idx/saved/elem/count permutations: 9-18), init-line ordering (count first vs last: 9), for-loop form (24, build_insns 85), inline success path (28), removing prologue_config entry (11), register-asm pins on idx/saved as diagnostic (still 9 — pins ignored, confirming strong RA preference). The target allocation needs idx@s3+arg0@s4 but cc1 naturally produces saved@s3+idx@s4+arg0@s5 — flipping requires either (a) a cheat-class lever to bump idx's RTL ref count without emitting bytes, (b) the FORBIDDEN DImode chain, or (c) source-level changes that all measured worse. Sibling func_80080258 and func_80080390 in same file have the IDENTICAL DImode chain + register-asm pin — they're also committed cheats under the new policy and need their own re-derivation. Resume avenues: (1) directed permuter from clean target (similar to cpu_side_move_dir_4 session 10) — high effort, low expected yield given the prior allocation-tie evidence on cpu_side_move_dir_4/marionation_Exec; (2) project-wide rodata layout change if the function's data adjacency suggests TU re-attribution (per jtbl-rodata-split-infrastructure evidence checklist); (3) instrumented cc1 (tmp/gccdbg with ALLOCDBG) probe to identify the SPECIFIC priority-flip C lever needed — same approach as cpu_side_move_dir_4 sessions 5-9 with the chain-extender finding (legitimate-form unclear). For now, function stays INCOMPLETE with the 4 regfix rules + DImode chain that build to oracle-matching SHA1; user/orchestrator policy call on whether/when to clean up the sibling cluster (func_80080258, func_80080390, tslPolyF4Init) together via canonical-asm authorization or further pure-C derivation.

Forbidden frame-coercion blocker under dead-vars-local-array; see memory/project/gnd-init-80041688-frame-coercion-evidence.md. HEAD uses volatile s32 sp10[8] plus (void)sp10 solely to reserve target frame 0x38; detector reports 1 cheat. Removing it gives cheat_count=0 but sandbox --disable all rises to distance 8: natural frame is 0x18 while target has no stack accesses beyond ra/s0 save/restore. Follow-up sweep 2026-06-05: RGB load-order rewrites stayed/worsened at score 8/15/16; semantic color structs/reg aggregates either wrong frame or extra stack traffic; exact-frame real aggregate locals hit frame 0x38 but emit stack stores absent from target (score 9, 87/82 insns). Diagnostic unused array matches frame with cheat_count=1; diagnostic unused struct also matches frame but is a detector loophole and the same forbidden phantom-local cheat. No SOTN-clean completion path found; leave incomplete until a real semantic frame source or policy/tooling decision exists.

Pure-C floor at sandbox-distance 4. Single 4-insn prologue reorder rule (regfix.txt:3753) flips save+def pairing from cc1's natural [sw s1; move s1,a0; sw s0; move s0,a1] to target's [sw s0; move s0,a1; sw s1; move s1,a0]. Mechanism: cc1's mips.c save_restore_insns iterates GP_REG_LAST->FIRST (3000a) and expand_function_start creates pseudos in DECL_ARGUMENTS order (out=72->s1, r=73->s0). Save-order is structurally fixed by mips machdep + ABI param order. Tried 9 distinct C source levers: early r-temps (3 forms), local alias, K&R-style decl, K&R-with-reversed-decl-order, var_a3=7 init, 3rd-callee-save force, all-temp-stores-at-end - all either CSE'd back or regressed. Sibling func_8007C4B8 has identical issue (twin function, same rule shape). Per [[no-compiler-divergence]] mips.c is frozen; per ABI param swap would break the asm callers. Either accept prologue_config.json as infrastructure (policy decision) or authorize ASM-region for the prologue-only debt.

prologue save-order structural — twin of just-parked func_8007C2A0 (2b3ccfe). Single 4-insn regfix reorder 3,4,1,2 @ 1-4 swaps cc1's natural [sw s1,52; move s1,a0; sw s0,48; move s0,a1] to target's [sw s0,48; move s0,a1; sw s1,52; move s1,a0]. Same identical mips.c save_restore_insns iteration-order wall, same identical C signature (s32 *out, Rect *r), same identical body shape. Honest sandbox=4 verified fixed across the 9 levers tested on the sibling + 1 additional probe (s16 rx,ry locals + tmp_v0 call-result temp) this session: same 4-distance floor. Path to COMPLETED-C identical to sibling: requires either function-scope canonical-asm wrapping of the prologue 4-insn block, treating regfix reorder as canonical infrastructure (like prologue_config.json's AddTbpOfst_80047EE8 family), or a novel C lever spanning the mips.c scheduling space the explored levers don't cover.

Session 3 (2026-06-02) re-park under SOTN-aligned policy (commit 161c6c3: defeat-licm var-reuse, opaque arithmetic, sub-word param read casts, mixed exit forms, duplicate-read into branch arms, named-intermediate declaration-order are all ALLOWED). Honest floor remains 6 via Lever B (named-intermediate u32 mask reuse). Tested 17 NEW structural variants this session applying the now-allowed levers: (v1) named mask + named addr -> 7 folded; (v3) u32 *p = ot pointer reuse -> 7 folded; (v4) goto end + mixed-exit on Lever B -> 6 same; (v5) opaque offset *(ot+zero) -> 6 (zero folded); (v6) duplicate-read into branch arms -> 22 regress (cross-jump-merge cost); (v7) u32 *ret_val = ot decl early -> 6 same; (v8) reuse single p for dev_table + return ptr -> 6 (CSE'd dead store); (v9) opaque arith 0xFFFFFF+1-1 -> 6 (constant folded); (v10) decl-order block-scoped result + mask -> 6 same; (v11) opaque addr arith (u32)&ot_end + (u32)zero -> 7 regress; (v12) hi|lo mask split -> 7 folded (lui+ori still single chain); (v13) opaque +1-1 on mask -> 6 folded; (v14) store inside dispatch block -> 20 regress; (v15) mask declared at top (pre-dispatch) -> 19 regress (mask spilled across call to callee-save); (v16) reuse n parameter for mask (defeat-licm var-reuse pattern) -> 17 regress (n lives in $s1 callee-save, mask cascades wrong); (v17) reuse v for (u32)dev_table + mask -> 10 regress BUT shows lever is real: move v0,s0 staged BEFORE store (target order!), but mask landed in $v1 not $a0 + addr in $v0 not $v1 (different cycle); (v18) keep vp scope + reuse v -> 6 (dead store DCE'd back to Lever B). Permuter ~12,845 iters from Lever B base (score 135), zero improvements (no candidate beat 135). MECHANISM CONFIRMED: target requires `move $v0, $s0` (return-staging) scheduled at idx 23 BEFORE the mask/addr chain (idx 24-26). cc1's sched.c gives return-staging priority 1 (chain depth to jr ra is 1); the mask/addr chain has priority 4 (lui->addiu->and->sw). Target's schedule requires return-staging priority >=4. The only way to extend the return-staging pseudo's chain depth in pure C is to make a later instruction (the store) consume the return-value register $v0 — which in turn requires GCC to keep `ret_val` as a separate pseudo from `ot` after copy-prop. No pure-C SOTN-allowed construct prevents this copy-prop (every tested `u32 *p = ot; *p = ...; return p;` form folds to ot). The previously-rejected closing forms (conditional dead-store, function-pointer return-type lie) both manipulate $v0 liveness differently and remain FORBIDDEN per the expanded cheat catalog. Function stays INCOMPLETE in queue; per [[no-new-park-categories]] no new park category requested. Honest floor 6 stable across 3 evidence-driven C-exhaustion sessions (50+ structural variants + ~50k permuter iters total).

Session-11 (2026-06-01): Tested 9 new structural variants from current-src baseline + permuter 2275 iters. All measured same or worse than baseline 11-aligned-diffs (sandbox 7 masked-Levenshtein). Permuter findings are all cheat-form (& 0xFFu / & 0xFFFFu masks, u16 type narrowing, variable repurposing via i/status aliases) — rejected per no-new-park-categories.md cheat-by-any-spelling policy. The byte chain register-allocation tie ( vs  for the arg4 chain temp) remains the gap — local-alloc qty_compare priority that no explored C-source structure flips without emitting bytes. C97C-class lever (m2c-shape + named intermediates) does NOT apply here (gap is not OR-association/sll-scheduling). Only un-tried avenue: PERM_*-directed permuter macros targeting the specific allocno slot — random-mode permuter exhausted. New rules loaded this session (goto-end-prologue-delay-slot, param-local-alias-prologue-pair-flip) do NOT apply — function has no early-exit + 4-insn-entry pattern and target uses natural ascending arg-save order (=a0, =a1 matches cc1 default). State unchanged: 5 regfix rules + chain lever + sandbox 7 distance. See [[register-alloc-pure-c]] for full session 5-10 evidence.

cross-jump merge DIRECTION wall: GCC merges case 1 and case 2 tails into a shared .L139 mini-block instead of target's case 2 -> case 1 backjump structure. Honest pure-C floor LOWERED 11 -> 10 (2026-06-01) via three combined levers documented in memory/project/func-8007ca00-m2c-shape-floor.md: (1) m2c-shape body adoption (single switch with shared block_5 label, default-at-end so it merges with shared tail); (2) var_a0 split (case 1 BE77!=0 pre-loads arg0[0] into var_a0 before goto block_5; case 2 BE77!=0 does the same); (3) 2-statement subtract { s32 tmp = 0x400 - var_v1; tmp -= var_a0; return tmp; } block-local form (vs single expression — controls subu order and keeps tmp in $v0). All pure-C, no cheats. Residual 10 diffs are reorg.c delay-slot-fill decisions (case 1 beqz delay should be li v0,1024 per target; case 2 BE77!=0 j delay should be li v0,1024 not sra v1,v1,1) and case 2 BE77==0 register chain ( vs ). Resume avenues (untried this session): directed permuter from new sandbox-10 base in permuter/ca00/; instrumented tmp/gccdbg/cc1 with BB2_FILL_DEBUG to find why reorg declines the li v0,1024 copy into beqz delay; rule UPDATE (the 13 existing rules are calibrated for the OLD body and can't retire on the new shape — need new rules for case 1 beqz delay fill + case 2 j delay fill + case 2 BE77==0 v0/v1 swap). NOT a documented auto-park category.
Session D 2026-06-02: SOTN-allowed duplicate-read lever lowers honest floor 16 to 12; structural ceiling = -vs- X-preserve tiebreaker; raw_arg0 chain reaches 10 but forbidden as literal-rename cheat

Honest pure-C floor 20 to 12 via SOTN duplicate-read lever (session 4 / 2026-06-02 post-161c6c3). Same structural ceiling as sibling C7A0 (missing target-idx-16 move a3,v0 park-merge; -vs- X-preserve register cascade). 21 rules in regfix.txt:3073-3094 do not accommodate the score-12 form. See memory/project/func-8007c86c-sotn-duplicate-read-lever.md.

New evidence (2026-06-01): m2c-shape adoption [var_v0 accumulator + nested if (rect[2]!=0) { if (rect[3]==0) return -1; ...body...; return call(); } + outer return -1;] reduces honest cheat-free distance 21 -> 7 (build_insns 49=49). Defeats cross-jump merge by mixing exit forms (TWO inline return -1 + one tail return-via-call) so jump2 cannot merge the early-return suffixes. Verified: bnez->body / j end / li v0,-1 split form emerges; sll v0,s1,16 fills bnez delay slot via reorg. The residual 7 diffs are PURE register-allocation choices the cc1 local_alloc makes: target lw a0,0(s0) for arg0[0] vs sandbox lw v0,0(s0); cascades to sw a0,0(a1) vs sw v0,0(a1). lreg dump (tmp/b6c8.i.lreg) shows pseudo 94 (arg0[0] temp) is a local allocno that local_alloc picks  for because  is freed by the BF28 store scheduled EARLY. Target keeps packed live in  across the load via LATE BF28 store scheduling (delays sw v0,(BF28) past arg0[0] load). Mechanism: GCC 2.7.2 sched2 priority for the BF28 store depends on its downstream dep chain depth (0 hops in cc1's natural form) - to delay it past arg0[0] requires either (a) extending the BF28 store's anti-dep chain so it cannot fire early, or (b) preventing local_alloc from picking  for pseudo 94. Adding any function-scope local (s32 packed; s32 a0_val; etc) breaks the early-return split (regresses to 21) due to frame-shift impact on jump2. NEXT untried levers: (1) declare bf24 as a callee-save register-class hinted pseudo to lengthen its live range so it cannot share with arg0[0]; (2) write arg0[0] read as a chained store-then-load through bf24+offset alias so the load-store dependency chain explicitly extends BF28's store priority; (3) probe BB2_SCHED_DEBUG/BB2_PRIO_DEBUG instrumented cc1 dump of THIS function (tmp/gccdbg/cc1 SHA1 a17fc6bbcb... May 30) to see exact INSN_PRIORITY of BF28 store vs arg0[0] load in the new structure; (4) restructure the call to take arg0[0] as a register-promoted arg (e.g. pass through a wrapper) that forces a0 allocation upstream of the body. The 19-rule cluster remains entirely register-renames after m2c-shape adoption; if any of these levers brings cc1 to pick  for arg0[0] naturally, all 19 rules collapse together.

global-label-drift-sibling-cheat + 4-reg-cluster coupling. Honest pure-C distance reduced 24->19 by removing 5 cheat-asm pins (////) and switching r/g to u32 (so r>>=3 emits srl matching target). Remaining gap: target keeps arg0 in  throughout body and uses /// as the scratch sequence with lui , 0xE200 placed LATE (after final lh , 6(a0)). Mine: GCC global-alloc puts arg0 in  because local-alloc gave  to r (first lbu dest), creating a hard 4-pseudo cluster cascade matching [[register-alloc-pure-c]] / [[split-read-defeats-hoist]] pure-RA-rename plateau signature. Tested: if-goto-null/if-non-zero/if-else/early-return forms, s32 vs u32 vs s32-coerced typing, consolidated single-line exprs, block-local var scope, decl reordering, dead-store/preload var (e2=0xE2000000 pulled the lui INTO the bne delay slot, regressing). All variations stayed >= 19. Critical coupling: any structural edit here shifts cc1's .L count and breaks sibling func_8007CE0C's hardcoded-.L<N> asmfix rules (link error on .L152/.L154/.L156/.L158 — confirmed via verify-oracle --rebuild after edit). Per [[global-label-drift-sibling-cheat]] this is a TWO-FUNCTION coupling: needs user policy decision to either (a) de-cheat func_8007CE0C's .L<N>-hardcoded rules first, or (b) bump sibling rules to new label numbers, or (c) find a pure-C lever that closes the cluster WITHOUT shifting cc1's label count. The 4-pseudo register-rotation matches the cpu_side_move_dir_4/marionation_Exec coupling pattern; the matching C exists but the explored lever space (if-form, type, order, scope) does not contain it.

Semantic frame investigation (memory/project/func-8007ce0c-semantic-investigation.md, 2026-06-01): 32-byte vars region (0x10-0x2F) in target frame=80 is entirely UNREFERENCED in func body — confirmed by reading every -relative access in asm/funcs/func_8007CE0C.s. cc1 emits frame=64 from any tested pure-C source; only forbidden patterns (s32 buf[N], (void)&local — both detected by engine/volatile_cheats) reproduce target frame size. NOT canonical-asm: scan_hand_coded tier=LOW score=1/8 (only S4); diagnose's CANONICAL-ASM verdict is false positive (mflo heuristic over-fires). Pure-C floor 41 reachable via compare-flip + drop cheat-asm pin (vs HEAD 48), but no rule retirement so not committed. Per [[no-new-park-categories]] no frame-size-infrastructure carve-out; per [[dead-vars-local-array]] no padding workaround. 23 rules remain (now drift-robust via {lbl#N} per db690e7, so this function is no longer a sibling-coupling blocker). REVISIT WHEN: new C lever discovered (e.g. via instrumented cc1 alloc/sched dumps in the BB2_*_DEBUG family), OR hand-coded-signal reassessment reveals evidence the scan_hand_coded heuristic missed.

register-alloc coupling — arg pseudos win allocno tiebreaker for low callee-saves (creation order), forcing rotation. chain-extender already present, volatile-bump regresses (cost > saved). same mechanism as cpu_side_move_dir_4 at larger surface; full pinning in register-alloc-pure-c.md.
